title: brickellia microphylla subsp. scabra
intro: Brickellia microphylla subsp. scabra, also known as Rough-leaf Brickellia, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its small, rough leaves and yellow or purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its rich composition of flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids. Consuming the plant has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial activity that can help combat infections. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as colds and bronchitis, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne.
